# ANALYSIS: EXPANDING_WINDOW_WALK_FORWARD_AND_REAL_FILLS_ON_J_DAYS

**Filed:** 2026-06-01  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** analysis  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We are testing the core BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON strategy's ability to capture the J anchor P&L using an expanding window walk-forward (train on all data prior to each J day, test on that J day) and validating with realistic OPRA real-fills simulation to assess whether the engine would have achieved the source-of-truth P&L on 4/29, 5/01, 5/04.

## Mechanism

For each J day (4/29, 5/01, 5/04):
1. **Expanding window walk-forward:** Train the engine on all SPY 5m data strictly prior to the J day (using current strategy parameters and rules from `automation/state/params.json` and `markdown/0dte/playbook.md`). Simulate engine decisions on the J day only, recording all trades and P&L.
2. **Real-fills simulation:** Re-run the engine's J day decisions using realistic OPRA fills (cached real bid/ask, slippage model) to compute actual P&L that would have been realized in live trading.
3. Compare engine P&L from both simulations to the anchor P&L (source-of-truth trade) for each day.

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Anchor P&L (source-of-truth) | Engine P&L (expanding window walk-forward) | Engine P&L (real-fills sim) | Delta (walk-forward vs anchor) | Delta (real-fills vs anchor) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | +$342 | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest |
| 5/01 winner | +$470 | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest |
| 5/04 winner | +$730 | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest |

(Note: Loser days 5/05, 5/06, 5/07 are not included in this expanding window/walk-forward real-fills simulation task per instructions. Edge_capture contribution from losers requires separate simulation.)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+ account; $1K paper account ~= 14% of headline P&L (per L11 in LESSONS-LEARNED.md).
2. **Sample bias:** Expanding window uses all prior data up to each J day; sample size grows per day (4/29: ~252 days prior, 5/01: ~253 days prior, 5/04: ~256 days prior). Selection method: deterministic expanding window. Overfit risk: low for walk-forward as each test day is unseen during training, but real-fills sim uses same OPRA cache for all days (may not capture intraday liquidity variation).
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (this analysis is purely in-sample expanding window; true OOS would require data after 5/04).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (real-fills simulation not yet executed; requires OPRA cache and realistic slippage model).
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: engine could miss all three J winners (edge_capture = -$1,542) if expanding window parameters fail to trigger entries.
   - Max drawdown: simulated equity curve could drop >50% on a single loser day if engine takes losing trades contrary to anchor.
   - Blow-up scenario: engine takes large losing trades on J loser days (e.g., 5/05 -$260, 5/06 -$300) while missing winners, causing edge_capture << 0.
6. **Concentration:** If engine captures all three J winners, top-3 days = 100% of edge_capture (by definition, as these are the only anchor days considered in this analysis).

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym validators: 67/67 PASS (current engine passes)
- Expanding window walk-forward: must complete for all three J days without errors
- Real-fills simulation: must run on OPRA cache with slippage model and show <20% diff from BS-sim for top-3 J days (per L12)
- OP-16 edge_capture >= 771 (must be verified after simulation)

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- <brief reasoning>
Low confidence due to absence of actual backtest results; requires Stage-1 execution to fill unknowns.
